sanskrit:
पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि वि भावसौ।
जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु॥
transliteration: puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛthivyāṃ ca tejaś cāsmi vibhāvasau | jīvanaṃ sarva-bhūteṣu tapaś cāsmi tapasviṣu ||
literal_meaning: I am the pure fragrance in earth, the brilliance in fire, the life-force in all beings, the austerity in those who practice it.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names the subtle, life-giving qualities behind matter and effort, inviting Arjuna to sense the sacred substrate that precedes every experience.
psychological_state: Heightened sensory openness; the mind shifts from object to essence, experiencing a pre-verbal sense of aliveness.
trigger_scenario: Walking into a pine forest after rain, suddenly noticing the scent of earth and feeling unmistakably 'awake'—wondering what inside you is doing the noticing.
anchor_text: Feel the unseen sacred essence that quietly animates every breath, flame, and grain of soil.
